role_summary: Sr. Director, Global Medical Affairs at Eli Lilly. Recently promoted from Sr. Director HCO Strategy. ~$500K comp, manages own IT org. Harvard Medical School, industrial design background, ICF PCC certified executive coach.
pain_themes: Fortune 500 pharma-grade IT lockdown (MDM, DLP, endpoint monitoring, zero-trust)
Cannot approve external MDM connection — needs higher IT authority
Needs to experience solution firsthand before championing internally
motivations: Leader enablement, not automation for its own sake
Systems thinker with design + coaching DNA
Wants to build personal Claw before making internal case
communication_style: Thoughtful, technical, systems-oriented. Respects directness. Won't bat for a solution he hasn't experienced gains from.
relationship_notes: Joel called Stephen 2/28, acknowledged getting ahead of himself. Stephen sees vision but needs firsthand experience. Three-part response: adversarial testing, personal Claw build, hourly engagement. NOT a stall — internal work required to become champion.
